title: Fix handling of Ambient/Inheritable caps for non root user
body:
On F33 we are getting permission denied when changing default
capabilities of containers.  I believe Ineritable and Ambient
capabilities have to match Permissited and potentially Effective.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: @baude this should fix the issue we have been seeing on F33.
I tried a scratch build with this patch and it doesn't give me the capset error anymore. So LGTM.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5*, *rhatdan*, *saschagrunert* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). alternative solution: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/7820

I've checked what Docker 19.03.12 does so now Podman sets the same capabilities
@giuseppe Have you tried yours against Fedora 33?
It seems like the capabilities system has had a few fairly major breakages in the recent releases. Are there tests that cover these cases, so they can be caught in the future by the CI?
@micah we are adding them,  But one of them was newer kernel with crun bug, which was hard to catch.
